# Implementation Checklist: Cztery Sensory Optymalnych Okien Sprzedazy Energii

**Purpose**: Validate whether the current spec, plan, and task breakdown are written clearly enough to support low-risk implementation without reopening design decisions.
**Created**: 2026-05-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Implementation Readiness

- [x] CHK001 Are the core implementation surfaces explicitly identified across plan and quickstart, so work does not begin with file-scope ambiguity? [Completeness, Plan §Project Structure]
- [x] CHK002 Is the shared ranking core described precisely enough that implementers know whether to refactor or replace the existing midday selector? [Clarity, Plan §Summary, Plan §Structure Decision]
- [x] CHK003 Are plan constraints explicit that the feature remains sell-only, additive, and non-destructive to existing sensors? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001a, Spec §FR-001b, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK004 Is the implementation boundary clear that pricing logic belongs in calculations and HA publication belongs in entity and registration files? [Clarity, Plan §Constitution Check, Plan §Structure Decision]

## Task Granularity

- [x] CHK005 Are foundational tasks small enough to be completed and reviewed independently without hiding multiple design decisions inside one item? [Clarity, Tasks §Phase 2]
- [x] CHK006 Do entity tasks distinguish identity concerns, state publication, and registration concerns clearly enough to avoid partial implementations? [Completeness, Tasks §Phase 2, Tasks §US1, Tasks §US2]
- [x] CHK007 Are translation updates treated as explicit implementation work rather than an implied side effect of entity creation? [Completeness, Tasks §T003, Tasks §T015, Tasks §T021]
- [x] CHK008 Are regression-related tasks separate from feature-delivery tasks strongly enough that reviewers can verify scope protection independently? [Traceability, Tasks §US3, Spec §FR-001a, Spec §SC-007]

## Dependency Ordering

- [x] CHK009 Does the task order enforce shared ranking and identity foundations before story-specific entity publication begins? [Consistency, Tasks §Phase 2, Tasks §Phase 3]
- [x] CHK010 Are today and tomorrow stories decomposed symmetrically enough that one does not silently depend on undocumented work from the other? [Consistency, Tasks §US1, Tasks §US2]
- [x] CHK011 Is the point at which existing-sensor coexistence must be preserved defined early enough to prevent destructive intermediate states? [Coverage, Tasks §Phase 2, Tasks §US3]
- [x] CHK012 Are polish-phase tasks limited to cross-cutting validation rather than hiding required core implementation work too late in the sequence? [Clarity, Tasks §Phase 6]

## Test Readiness

- [x] CHK013 Are story-local tests specified clearly enough that each user story can be judged independently before moving to the next one? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Tasks §US1, Tasks §US2, Tasks §US3]
- [x] CHK014 Do task descriptions make it clear which behaviors belong in pure calculation tests versus HA-facing sensor publication tests? [Clarity, Plan §Testing, Tasks §T010, Tasks §T011, Tasks §T016, Tasks §T017, Tasks §T022, Tasks §T023]
- [x] CHK015 Are regression assertions for unchanged existing sensors visible early enough in US1 and US2 to support the claimed independent tests? [Traceability, Spec §FR-001a, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Tasks §T011, Tasks §T017]
- [x] CHK016 Is broader coexistence and registration regression coverage preserved as a distinct late hardening step rather than diluted into basic story tests? [Consistency, Tasks §T023, Tasks §T024, Tasks §T027]
- [x] CHK017 Are final validation commands aligned with the planned test files, rather than requiring extra discovery during implementation? [Completeness, Quickstart §Validation, Tasks §T030]

## Identity And Naming Readiness

- [x] CHK018 Are translation-backed naming requirements explicit enough in the implementation tasks that entity identity cannot be postponed or forgotten? [Traceability, Plan §Constraints, Tasks §T009]
- [x] CHK019 Do the tasks distinguish minimal identity verification in story-local tests from broader identity regression in US3 clearly enough to support incremental delivery? [Clarity, Tasks §T011, Tasks §T017, Tasks §T023]
- [x] CHK020 Is config-entry-scoped `unique_id` behavior specified strongly enough in plan and tasks to avoid accidental reuse of static IDs? [Coverage, Plan §Constraints, Tasks §T009, Tasks §T011, Tasks §T017, Tasks §T023]

## Scope Protection

- [x] CHK021 Are sell-only scope protections written strongly enough that future buy-window work cannot slip into this feature through code, translation, or test edits? [Coverage, Spec §FR-001b, Tasks §T029]
- [x] CHK022 Does the implementation documentation define enough surfaces for scope review to include calculations and translations, not only entity registration and tests? [Gap, Plan §Project Structure, Quickstart §Suggested File Touches]
- [x] CHK023 Are additive-scope safeguards described as implementation requirements rather than optional polish or reviewer preference? [Traceability, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-001a, Tasks §Phase 2, Tasks §US3]

## Delivery Readiness

- [x] CHK024 Can an implementer begin Phase 2 without reopening unresolved questions about file ownership, data shape, time ranges, or output format? [Traceability, Spec §FR-003a, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-007, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK025 Are checkpoints written as meaningful go/no-go review points rather than restatements of phase titles? [Clarity, Tasks §Checkpoints]
- [x] CHK026 Is the MVP definition narrow enough that User Story 1 can ship value without implicitly requiring later hardening work to become usable? [Measurability, Tasks §Implementation Strategy, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK027 Do parallel-work notes avoid creating hidden file conflicts or overlapping ownership on the same implementation surfaces? [Consistency, Tasks §Parallel Opportunities, Tasks §Parallel Team Strategy]
- [x] CHK028 Does the current artifact set provide enough implementation readiness to proceed without generating another planning document? [Traceability, Spec §Clarifications, Plan §Structure Decision, Tasks §Implementation Strategy]

## Notes

- This is a standard-rigor, author-facing checklist focused on implementation readiness across spec, plan, quickstart, and tasks.
- It intentionally evaluates the quality of implementation planning, not whether the code already works.
